slides.headline: Convert clock to credit hours with ease
slides.body: Understand the nuances of clock hour to credit hour conversions and how they impact VA reporting for tech schools.

slides.headline: Understand the conversion formula
slides.body: Learn the standard formula: credit hours = clock hours / 1.75. Example: 20 clock hours = 20 / 1.75 = 11.43 credit hours.
bullets: Apply the formula: credit hours = clock hours / 1.75

slides.headline: Account for rounding
slides.body: VA requires rounding to the nearest whole number. Example: 11.43 rounds up to 12 credit hours.
bullets: Round up to nearest whole number

slides.headline: VA reporting requirements
slides.body: VA requires courses to be listed as credit hours, not clock hours. Ensure all courses are reported as credit hours for compliance.

slides.headline: Common pitfalls
slides.body: Misinterpreting the conversion formula can lead to discrepancies. Always double-check calculations and rounding.
bullets: Misinterpreting the formula can cause errors

slides.headline: Stay compliant
slides.body: Ensure all clock hour courses are accurately converted to credit hours and reported to the VA. Avoid potential penalties.
Learn more about clock hour to credit hour conversions and VA reporting requirements. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Navigate the clock hour to credit hour conversion maze with clarity and ease. Understand the formula, avoid common pitfalls, and ensure VA compliance. Learn more: Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3, as it could benefit from a more engaging headline to draw attention.
